rulings.Case Title: JACK BARLEY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 30, 2008, line 1. DEFENDANT CRANE CO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant shifted the burden of producing evidence based on the Merrick Declaration, special interrogatories and plaintiff's deposition testimony. Plaintiff raised a triable issue of fact on exposure based on plaintiff's declaration explaining the basis for work on original equipment. The Court grants summary adjudication on the issue of punitive damages and declines to do so with respect to component and affixed parts because such a ruling would not resolve a cause of action. Defendant's evidentiary objections 9 and 10 are sustained and the others are overruled. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD BOWMAN et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 30, 2008, line 2. DEFENDANT PACIFIC COAST BUILDING PRODUCTS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of producing evidence by asking specific deposition questions designed to obtain all information plaintiff had regarding defendant. Plaintiff's answers were very clear that he had no information and knew of no records or persons who could support his claim. Plaintiff failed to present any evidence giving rise to a triable issue of fact. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: GERALD BROOKS,SR. VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 30, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANT KYLE RAILWAYS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant failed to shift the burden of proof because defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff knew that "Kyle Railways, Inc" was his employer as contrasted with "California Western Railroad" when the complaint was filed. Plaintiff's deposition testimony is ambiguous as to when he knew defendant was his employer. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: RYAN PARK VS. DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Iof Mandate And/Or Prohibition Or Other Appropriate Relief
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 30, 2008, line 16. PETITIONER RYAN PARK'S Petition For Writ Iof Mandate And/Or Prohibition Or Other Appropriate Relief. The Writ is denied. The decision in Fearn v. Zolin (1992) 9 Cal. App. 4th 1756, makes clear that the DMV is responsible for suspending plaintiff's license based on the criminal conviction and the administrative proceeding. The criminal suspension does not relate back to plaintiff's release on bail but begins only after the conviction. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: EMPORIUM MALL L.L.C. A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. M&J INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 30, 2008, line 17. CROSS DEFENDANT EMPORIUM MALL LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained in part with leave to amend and overruled in part. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend to plead facts establishing a basis for Lee's individual claim and to plead specific facts to support the misrepresentation claim. Overruled as to breach of contract and breach of covenant causes of action. =(302/PJM/PB)
